subject: literature-in-english
question: Romeo and Juliet is a tragedy because

options.A: it is full of noblemen and ladies
options.D: Juliet is only fourteen years old

explanation:
because both romeo and juliet died untimely in the play.
a tragedy is a play dealing with tragic events and having an unhappy ending, especially one concerning the downfall of the main character.
passage: From the novel; Romeo and Juliet

subject: literature-in-english
question: Essentially,plays are meant

options.A: to be read for sheer pleasure
options.C: to be presented on stage through words and actions
options.D: to keep people out of trouble

explanation: A play is a form of dramatic literature that is specifically written to be performed rather than just read. It uses dialogue , actions , stage directions and character interaction to convey its message and entertain or educate an audience. Plays are staged in theatres with actors performing the script. The visual and auditory elements (movement, tone, set design) are essential to their full impact.

question: In 'Fulani Cattle' J.P Clark expresses

options.A: the anger and sympathy he feels at seeing cattle driven all the way from the North to the South of Nigeria to be slaughtered
options.B: admiration for the beauty of Fulani cattle
options.C: grief at the gradual destruction of this fine breed of cattle
options.D: the joy of the cattle at being driven over pleasant fields
options.E: the idea that more cattle should be taken to the South to be slaughtered

subject: literature-in-english
question: Dinny,Lippy,Steepy and Fatty are characters in

subject: literature-in-english
question: In one of the three jugs The three jugs were on certain evenings to return the tranquil souls, the breaths of the ancestors, The ancestors who were men, The ancestors who were sages, Mother has dipped three fingers three fingers of her left hand: thumb, forefinger and middle finger I have dipped three fingers Three fingers of my right hand: thumb, forefinger and middle finger. (From "Viaticum"by B. Diop) In this poem, the repetitive pattern suggests

options.B: ritual or established form of worship
options.C: the love of a mother for her child
options.D: the beauty of the full moon
options.E: the glorious lives of our ancestors

explanation:
The poem "Viaticum" by B. Diop uses repetition , particularly in the actions of dipping three fingers and the naming of those fingers, to evoke a ceremonial or sacred atmosphere . This kind of repetition is characteristic of rituals or traditional forms of worship , especially when tied to ancestral reverence.
The mention of ancestors , tranquil soul and the specific, repeated action involving dipping fingers all indicate a ritualistic gesture . Such patterns are common in traditional African religious or spiritual practices, where actions are symbolic , deliberate and repetitive to maintain sacred form.
